The question: "Does Biochemistry study gluons?" We need to determine if biochemistry includes the study of gluons. Biochemistry is the study of chemical processes within living organisms. Gluons are subatomic particles that mediate the strong nuclear force between quarks; they are part of particle physics and quantum chromodynamics. Typically not part of biochemistry, which focuses on biomolecules like proteins, nucleic acids, carbohydrates, lipids, etc. Gluons are not directly studied in biochemistry; they belong to high-energy physics.
